title: Executive Order 11327—Assigning Authority to Order Certain Persons in the Ready Reserve to Active Duty

flags.justification: Delegates authority to call up a narrowly and specifically defined category of Ready Reservists (three objective conditions in Sec. 1), not open-ended authority.
Clear, specific eligibility criteria plus Sec. 3's explicit fairness-consideration requirements (family responsibilities, essential employment).

Sec. 3's fairness-consideration factors ('appropriate consideration' for family responsibilities and essential employment) have no defined weighting process, even though the eligibility criteria themselves are objective.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
An explicit, clean expiration date is set: authority runs 'until June 30, 1968.'
No existing oversight mechanism is removed.
The narrow, objective eligibility criteria and explicit fairness-consideration requirement provide real, if imperfectly proceduralized, structure.
A narrow, time-limited, criteria-bound delegation; no broad precedent is claimed.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
